item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 45, rescuers search rubble
item.description: A powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck southern Philippines, killing at least 45 people and displacing 32,000. Rescuers continue searching collapsed buildings in General Santos city for survivors.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Kakao workers stage strike demanding job security
item.description: Unionized workers at South Korean tech giant Kakao began a four-hour strike demanding job security and changes to the bonus system. The action highlights growing labor tensions in the country's tech sector.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey proposes 2035 global electricity target at climate summit
item.description: As host of this year's global climate summit, Turkey proposed targeting electricity to meet 35% of world energy demand by 2035, aiming to cut fossil fuel consumption. The initiative seeks to accelerate clean energy transition.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru election remains too close to call as markets rally
item.description: Peru's presidential election remains on a knife edge between conservative Keiko Fujimori and leftist candidate Sanchez, with vote counting potentially taking up to two weeks. Markets rallied amid the uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US trade deficit narrows as petroleum exports hit record high
item.description: The US trade deficit narrowed in April as petroleum product exports reached record levels. If sustained, trade could contribute to economic growth in the second quarter despite global tensions.

item.title: ⚔️ US Launches Strikes on Iran After Apache Helicopter Downed Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military conducted retaliatory strikes against Iran after an Apache helicopter was shot down over the Strait of Hormuz, further straining a fragile ceasefire between Iran and Israel. President Trump confirmed the pilots survived and vowed proportional responses to Iranian aggression.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Producer Inflation Hits Highest Level in Nearly 4 Years
item.description: Chinas producer prices rose for a third straight month in May, reaching the highest since July 2022, while consumer prices also climbed. The data signals strengthening factory-gate inflation as Beijing continues economic stimulus measures.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇦 Germany Pledges €300M to Czech Ammunition Initiative for Ukraine
item.description: Germany will contribute an additional 300 million euros to the Czech ammunition initiative, approximately 50,000 rounds of artillery shells, as Ukraine continues to face ammunition shortages on the frontlines.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸🚢 South Korea and US Align on Nuclear-Powered Submarine Development
item.description: Seoul and Washington share the view that domestically built nuclear-powered submarines would strengthen South Korea's defense capabilities amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇰🇷⚠️🛸 South Korea Warned on Fragmented Air Defenses Against Drone Swarms
item.description: Military and academic experts warn South Korea must urgently build an integrated air and missile defense system to counter emerging drone swarm threats.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US Congress Approves $70 Billion Bill for Trump Immigration Crackdown
item.description: The US Congress passed a $70 billion funding bill for President Trump's immigration agenda, significantly expanding enforcement capabilities.

item.title: 🇭🇰💼📉 AI and Market Shifts Leave Hong Kong Graduates Facing Toughest Job Hunt Since 2021
item.description: Graduates are applying for hundreds of positions with few responses as entry-level roles vanish and companies turn to AI automation in Hong Kong's competitive job market.

item.title: 🇧🇬🇺🇦 Bulgaria Halts Arms Supplies to Ukraine Under New Government
item.description: Bulgaria newly appointed defense minister announced the country will no longer provide arms to Ukraine and urged Moscow and Kyiv to negotiate, marking a significant policy shift for a country that was a major ammunition supplier.

item.title: 🇨🇳🚢 China Coast Guard Harasses Commercial Shipping Near Taiwan
item.description: Chinese coast guard ships around Taiwan have been demanding commercial vessels disclose their origin and destination while claiming jurisdiction, Taiwan coast guard said, escalating maritime tensions in the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: 🇺🇸💻 US Senators Urge Tighter Rules on Contract Chipmakers Supplying China
item.description: A bipartisan pair of senators urged the Trump administration to tighten rules on chip contract manufacturers like TSMC to prevent them from making advanced AI chips for overseas subsidiaries of Chinese companies.

item.title: 🚁 Iranian Armed Drone Shot Down US Apache Helicopter, Sources Confirm
item.description: An Iranian Shahed drone struck the US Apache helicopter on patrol over the Strait of Hormuz. The two pilots were rescued within two hours. Shahed drones have been used extensively by Iran and supplied to Russia for the Ukraine war.

item.title: 🏛️ Congress Backs Iran War Powers Resolutions for First Time
item.description: For the first time since the Iran war began, the Republican-led Congress has backed resolutions that could block Trump from continuing hostilities, reflecting growing concern within his party about the three-month conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Considers Stricter AI Chip Export Controls to Align With US
item.description: Taiwan authorities are considering tighter export controls on AI chip sales to China to further align with US restrictions, amid growing technology competition between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Japan to Name China as Biggest Defense Concern in New Document
item.description: Japan Defense Ministry expected to designate China as the most serious concern in its annual defense document, reflecting growing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚛️ Iran to Dilute Uranium Stockpile Rather Than Hand Over in US Deal
item.description: Iran will dilute its uranium stockpile rather than hand it over as part of negotiations with the US, while talks focus on four key issues including halting Iranian uranium enrichment.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚠️ Violent Threats Against US Lawmakers Spiked After Meta Eased Moderation
item.description: A watchdog report found explicit calls for murder and sexual harassment targeting US lawmakers on Facebook increased after Meta relaxed content moderation policies.

item.title: 💥⚠️ Commentary: Middle East Hostilities a Question of When, Not If
item.description: Analysis suggests that despite temporary pauses in fighting between Israel and Iran, renewed hostilities remain likely as both sides leave the door open for escalation.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US House Passes $70 Billion Immigration Enforcement Package
item.description: The Republican-controlled House passed a $70 billion bill to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol through the remainder of President Trumps administration, ending a longstanding political stalemate over immigration funding.

item.title: 🇷🇺⛽ Fuel Supply Disruptions Trigger Panic-Buying in Southern Russia
item.description: Disruptions to fuel supplies triggered panic-buying in Russias Krasnodar region, the governor said, as Ukrainian strikes on energy infrastructure hit fuel deliveries across several southern regions and Russian-held Crimea.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Saskatchewan Marshals Service Poaching Officers from RCMP and Municipal Police Forces
item.description: New provincial marshal service accused of draining talent from existing RCMP and municipal police as it builds its ranks, with 25 of 27 officers reportedly recruited from other forces.
